name: Buttermilk-Apricot Scones
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Margarine or butter, chilled (and cut into small pieces)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, combine the dry ingredients: flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt.
2. Cut in the chilled margarine (or butter) using a pastry blender or two forks until the mixture resembles coarse meal.
3. Add the chopped dried apricots and toss well to ensure each piece is coated evenly with flour.
4. Make a well in the center of the mixture.
5. Combine the buttermilk, apricot nectar, and egg in another bowl.
6. Pour the liquid ingredients into the well and mix with the dry ingredients until just combined (dough will be sticky).
7. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface. Knead the dough 4-5 times or knead it directly in the bowl.
8. Pat the dough into a 9-inch circle on a baking sheet coated with cooking spray.
9. Cut the dough into 8 wedges.
10. Bake at 400°F for 15 minutes, or until golden brown.
11. Serve warm.
Each scone will be around 230 calories (30% from fat) more or less, depending on the fat content of the buttermilk. Yogurt works well as a substitute in the recipe. Optionally, brush the top of the scones with beaten egg white and sprinkle with sugar before baking for added sweetness.
